history: The client has a history of a traumatic incident involving sexual assault by her partner Ivan. She is currently navigating the complexities of rebuilding trust and intimacy in their relationship. She uses learning and teaching mathematics as a cognitive escape and metaphor for her personal journey.
unlovable_belief: I am bound to be abandoned
I am bound to be rejected
intermediate_belief: If I cannot control the situation, then I am vulnerable and unsafe. If I am vulnerable, then I will be hurt again.
intermediate_belief_depression: If I cannot control the situation, then I will never feel safe or secure.
coping_strategies: The client uses engagement with mathematics as a structured environment to control variables and outcomes, avoiding emotionally charged issues. She also uses humor and self-awareness to manage underlying tension.
situation: Physical intimacy is resuming in the relationship.
auto_thought: What if I get hurt again? I can't fully trust him yet.
behavior: She becomes self-conscious and retreats into her 'little bubble' of mathematics, avoiding direct confrontation of her emotions.
